growth_records.medium: CDM-LAB (chemically defined medium for lactic acid bacteria)

up.method: calculated: qS = D x S0 / dW = 0.357 x 55.5 mM / 1.83 g/L (glucose fully consumed, feed 55.5 mM)

yields.Yxs: 0.183 g biomass/g glucose (1.83 g/L dW / 10.0 g/L glucose; 55.5 mM x 180.16 g/mol = 10.0 g/L)

product_yields.note: lactate yield = 91.78 mM / 55.5 mM = 1.653 mol/mol; homolactic at high growth rate; dominating products lactate>formate>acetate>=ethanol

growth_records.cite: McLeod A et al. Effects of glucose availability in Lactobacillus sakei; metabolic change and regulation of the proteome and transcriptome. PLoS ONE (2017). DOI:10.1371/journal.pone.0187542. [PMC5669474]
growth_records.snippet: 23K ... 0.357 ... dW 1.83 (0.01) g/liter; Conc(mM) lactate 91.78, formate 15.13, acetate 10.57, ethanol 7.91; Flux(mmol/h)dW-1 17.84, 2.94, 2.06, 1.54; %C balance 98.32. The CDM-LAB medium contained 55.5 mM glucose.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state so mu=D=0.357 /h (calculated). doubling_time=ln2/mu=0.693/0.357=1.94 h. Secretion fluxes taken directly from Table 1 'Flux (mmol h-1) dW-1' row = specific production rate mmol/gDW/h. Verified consistency: Flux = D x Conc / dW; e.g. lactate 0.357 x 91.78 / 1.83 = 17.90 ~ 17.84 (reported). Glucose uptake computed qS = D x S0 / dW = 0.357 x 55.5 mM / 1.83 g/L = 10.83 mmol/gDW/h (glucose fully consumed; feed 55.5 mM stated in Table 1 footnote b). Yxs = 1.83 g/L / 10.0 g/L glucose = 0.183 g/g. Full CDM-LAB composition not given in text (cited refs 24,25), only glucose 55.5 mM stated.

product_yields.note: lactate yield = 76.25 mM / 55.5 mM = 1.374 mol/mol; shift towards mixed-acid at low growth rate (formate +49.3% vs high)

growth_records.snippet: 23K ... 0.045 ... dW 1.26 (0.09) g/liter; Conc(mM) lactate 76.25, formate 29.85, acetate 20.25, ethanol 15.28; Flux(mmol/h)dW-1 2.72, 1.06, 0.72, 0.54; %C balance 99.00. CDM-LAB contained 55.5 mM glucose.
growth_records.notes: Chemostat steady-state: mu=D=0.045 /h. doubling_time=0.693/0.045=15.4 h. Secretion fluxes = Table 1 Flux row (mmol/gDW/h). Check: lactate 0.045 x 76.25 / 1.26 = 2.72 (matches). Glucose qS = 0.045 x 55.5 / 1.26 = 1.98 mmol/gDW/h (glucose fully consumed). Yxs = 1.26/10.0 = 0.126 g/g. More mixed-acid fermentation at low growth rate.
